NVDA.reasoning: 4 of 5 strategy agents are bullish on NVDA; only swing_breakout is neutral (not bearish — it simply has no valid coiling setup because the squeeze already fired on the $164→$217 move). This is a buy-the-pullback-in-trend thesis, not a breakout or fade trade. The regime context is strongly supportive: ADX at 54.67 is one of the strongest trend readings in the market, daily EMA alignment is fully bullish (EMA10 204.98 > EMA21 197.47 > EMA50 190.15), and ROC across all timeframes (5d +3.33%, 10d +5.22%, 21d +26.69%) is accelerating. Price has pulled back 3.5% from the $216.83 52-week high to $209.25, resting near the hourly Fib 38.2% ($209.70) and volume-confirmed hourly support at $208.20 (15 tests). This is the textbook buy-the-dip-in-strong-trend setup. The primary catalyst is live: hyperscaler AI capex disclosure week (Alphabet, Meta, MSFT, Amazon Q1 earnings) is in-window, with each confirmed GPU spend increment a direct NVDA revenue signal. Macro regime is risk-on (S&P +9.3% in April, best month since 2020) driven by exactly the AI hardware thematic that benefits NVDA. The macro agent's asymmetric R/R is 3.5:1 (10.1% up / 2.9% down), well above the 2:1 minimum. The 4-agent consensus on the entry zone ($209.25–$210.00) and stop ($204.00–$206.50) is tight, with two agents independently confirming $205.30 as the critical Fib 61.8% stop level. Confidence is 74 (not higher) because: (1) RSI-21 at 81.52 is extended — Branch A mean-reversion criteria nearly fired; (2) hourly MACD histogram is negative (-0.59) and hourly OBV is trending down, confirming the pullback is still in progress; (3) relative volume is below average (0.84x daily, 0.56x hourly), meaning no fresh institutional buy confirmation yet — entry discipline at the $208.20–$210.00 zone is required.
NVDA.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is between swing_breakout (neutral, confidence 30) and the four bullish agents. This is a setup-filter disagreement, not a directional disagreement: breakout explicitly acknowledges the bull-flag continuation scenario but cannot assign a buy signal because the squeeze has already released (BBW 0.2591 is wide, volume is contracting, no new coiling detected). The four bullish agents are all playing the same post-breakout pullback continuation — the breakout agent just lacks the right tool for that regime. A secondary conflict exists on targets: macro_context projects $231.13 (the full Fib 1.272 extension of the $164–$217 major swing), while trend_momentum, mean_reversion, and catalyst_news cluster tightly at $221.89–$222.50 (the swing high retest plus modest extension). The $231.13 target is structurally valid for a longer holding period but is optimistic for a 5–13 day swing window. The synthesized target of $221.89 uses the cluster median and is the more defensible near-term objective. Stops are broadly consistent: trend_momentum and mean_reversion both pin $205.30 (hourly Fib 61.8%), catalyst_news uses $206.50 (above prev-day low), and macro uses $204.00 (daily EMA10 invalidation). The synthesized stop of $205.30 is the most commonly cited level and is structurally anchored at the Fib 61.8% retracement, which is the correct pullback invalidation point within a bull trend.
NVDA.exit_constraint: Must exit by approximately May 17, 2026 (3-day pre-earnings blackout before Q1 FY2027 earnings ~May 20-27)
